topic: NH heat excess asymmetry — change-driven fingerprint reconciliation + new TLDR/detail magnitude-ratio inconsistency
change_summary: tldr_evidence_length grew from 371 to 418 (+12.7%); tldr_verdict_length grew from 193 to 269 (+39.4%); detail_evidence_length grew from 1628 to 1635 (+0.4%); sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-28T01:09:01Z (68h old, fresh); obe_skipped=3 (WIN-006 reviewed at c6 2026-05-28T04:18, WIN-004 at c7 2026-05-29T07:15, WIN-029 at c5 2026-05-29T15:20 — all post-audit). KEY FINDING: the flagged tldr drift is a c5-fingerprint-update artifact, not real text change. The current tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict landed at workspace-sync commit d22f6b13 (2026-05-09T14:05Z) as part of the ISS-1906 closure (decider-self-apply 2026-05-09T13:20Z). c5 was written at 2026-05-09T22:46Z — AFTER the new TLDR was already in main — yet its text_fingerprint stored the pre-ISS-1906 values (371/193). The TLDR content has been byte-stable for 21 days; only the audit baseline was wrong. The c5 reviewer apparently fingerprinted from a stale clone or pre-rebase data snapshot before writing.
our_argument_summary: Verdict Std Model Explains continues to hold. Earth hemispheric energy balance is near-symmetric (Stephens 2012, doi:10.1038/ngeo1580); the +0.34 W/m^2/decade NH-SH absorbed-solar-radiation trend (Loeb 2025 PNAS, doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122) is an aerosol/albedo/cloud-driven 24-year trend, not a static asymmetry. The dome supplies no radiation-budget derivation; under the inverse-cube projected-flux integral from h=5,733 km geometry, the predicted NH-SH difference is ~90-270 W/m^2 across the dome's seasonal sun-orbit radius (~7,400 km solstice north to ~12,600 km solstice south) — wrong by ~250-800×. The defender-pivot paragraph in detail_verdict_text traps a dome advocate retreating to sign-only-prediction between three counters (sign-non-discrimination via Loeb 2025 mechanism quote; magnitude-disclaim against dome's own +0.34 citation; no-derivation dilemma). Since c5 (2026-05-09), the substantive prose is byte-identical; the c5 carry-over of ISS-1908 (range-width underived) appears to have been a misread — ISS-1908 was closed at 2026-05-09T13:20Z and the seasonal sun-orbit derivation (~7,400 km to ~12,600 km) is present in current detail_evidence.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.6 baseline; WIN-066 not present — V51 addition; unchanged since c5)
raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (V51 capture remains WIN-064-only as of 2026-05-08; WIN-066 V51 prose still not in raw-text corpus — c5 hole #2 source-verification concern persists)
monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-066.json (analyst confirms no monitor.py / repo code references WIN-066, hemispheric energy, CERES, or 0.34)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c2/c3/c4/c5: no dome-side calculation pipeline for WIN-066. monitor.py has no domain entry. No CERES pull, no hemispheric integral, no W/m^2 comparison logic. The dome publishes the WIN as a confirmed prediction in V51.0 by adopting the published CERES-derived figure.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — no validation logic of any kind exists for this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None observed in dome code. No dome-side change since c5.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. monitoring=none, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false, relabels_standard=true all remain correctly tagged. Verified against current data/wins.json:066.code_analysis.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-confirmed against fresh clone: no entry in raw-text/monitor.py for hemispheric energy, NH/SH ratio, CERES, or 0.34 W/m^2. WIN-066 is V51-additions only and the dome has not added a monitor.py domain since c5.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Stephens et al. 2012 (Nature Geoscience, doi:10.1038/ngeo1580) baselined hemispheric symmetry. Loeb et al. 2025 (PNAS, doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122) attributes the small NH-SH trend specifically to aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes — entirely standard-physics drivers. The dome contributes no new numerical prediction; it adopts +0.34 W/m^2/decade and reframes geometric asymmetry as the cause. Unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Loeb et al. 2025 published 2025-09-29 (PNAS issue 40), well before V51.0 (2026). CERES TOA flux datasets publicly indexed since ~2000. No monitor.py entry exists for the value, so the weaker commit-timestamp test is inapplicable. detail_verdict_text correctly invokes Part 3.1b carve-out. Unchanged from c5.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome formula starts from disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, or any aether parameter and arrives at +0.34 W/m^2/decade. Counter (3) of the defender-pivot paragraph captures this with the dilemma framing (own the inverse-cube integral or own no prediction at all). Unchanged from c5.

holes_found.description: NEW (c5 missed): TLDR-vs-detail magnitude-ratio inconsistency. tldr_verdict says "200–800× the actual value" while detail_verdict_text says "~250–800×". The arithmetic: 90/0.34 = 264.7 (lower bound of dome over-prediction) and 270/0.34 = 794.1 (upper bound), so the correct ratio range is ~265-794× — which the detail_verdict_text rounding to "250-800×" captures, but the tldr_verdict's "200-800×" understates the lower bound by ~25% (200× corresponds to 68/0.34, but the detail_evidence states the floor is 90 W/m^2, not 68 W/m^2). A defender skimming both TLDR and detail will catch the inconsistency: "Your TLDR claims 200× minimum; your derivation gives 265× minimum. Which is it?" The understatement is in the safe direction (it doesn't hurt our argument) but the cross-field inconsistency is rhetorically untidy and was introduced when ISS-1906 was self-applied. c5 did not flag this — it noted detail_verdict_text's "250-800×" as consistent with the c4 100-300 W/m^2 integration but did not cross-check against the tldr_verdict's framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten tldr_verdict's "200-800×" to "250-800×" to match detail_verdict_text and the arithmetic; OR replace with a generic phrase like "hundreds of times the actual value" (matching tldr_evidence's wording) so the TLDR doesn't commit to a specific lower-bound multiplier. The first fix is cleaner and preserves the punch; the second avoids future drift if the inverse-cube range tightens further.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #1 (still applies): Counter (1) of the defender-pivot paragraph appeals to globe-physics mechanisms (NH ~39% land vs SH ~19% land, albedo asymmetry, aerosol-radiation interactions per Loeb 2025) to argue NH > SH sign is non-discriminating. A sophisticated dome defender could still call this circular: "You are using globe-physics albedo arguments to refute a dome model. Of course standard climate science predicts NH > SH if you accept the underlying globe physics — but I am contesting that physics applies in the dome geometry." Counter (3) carries the dilemma squeeze; Counter (1) on its own would be vulnerable. No text change since c5, so this minor finding persists unchanged.
holes_found.recommendation: Same as c5: optional small tightening — make the empirical anchor explicit by phrasing as "directly observable continental land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH continental coverage)" or add half-sentence noting the land-mass percentages are direct measurements independent of the globe/dome geometry dispute. Optional, not blocking.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #2 (still applies): Counter (2) asserts "The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m^2/decade) as confirmation, not any positive NH-SH difference." The raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (2026-05-08 capture) is WIN-064-only; raw-text/02-wins.txt (V50.6 baseline 2026-03-12) predates WIN-066. We still do not have a verbatim V51 capture of the WIN-066 dome page text on file. Source-of-truth verification remains unsatisfied as of 2026-05-30. detail_claim and prior reviewer documentation both assert the dome cites +0.34, so Counter (2) is consistent with our best evidence, but a future poller capture of raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win066.txt would let us link Counter (2) to a verbatim source rather than to internal corroboration.
holes_found.recommendation: Surface this as a poller / analyst attention item: request a V51 capture for WIN-066 specifically on the next dome-page scrape. If the dome page does cite +0.34 verbatim, link Counter (2) to that source. If it does not, soften Counter (2) to "cites the published +0.34 W/m^2/decade as the confirming value." Not blocking on this review.

holes_found.description: CARRIED FORWARD from c5 hole #4 (still applies, no action): Cross-WIN sign-only systemic flag — sign-match-non-discrimination is a recurring failure mode across WIN-058/-059/-060 (NMP/SAA drift directional matches) and WIN-066. The defender-pivot paragraph closes the loophole for WIN-066 specifically but does not establish a named cross-WIN audit section. Each per-WIN closure is independently sound; the cost is rhetorical-economy not correctness. No change since c5. Cross-checked EXP-337-cross-win-sign-only-sweep.json exists in monitor/analyst/expansions/ — so this work is already queued. Confirming it has not yet been integrated.
holes_found.recommendation: No change required for c6. EXP-337 already addresses this as a planned cross-WIN sweep. If EXP-337 has not yet integrated by c8 of this WIN, escalate to the analyst attention-inbox.

stronger_arguments.description: Static-vs-trend critique remains under-emphasized in TLDRs (carry-forward from c5). The deeper point — a static dome geometry structurally cannot produce a 24-year trend at all (the dome geometry has no time-varying parameter that explains a per-decade increase) — is buried in detail_evidence ("driven by aerosol changes, Arctic ice loss, and cloud shifts — not a static hemispheric difference") but not crystallized as a named third failure layer alongside post-hoc and over-prediction. A defender who escapes both the magnitude and the sign-non-discrimination traps still falls into this one. Worth promoting to a labeled third counter ("Counter (4): static dome geometry cannot produce a per-decade trend") if EXP-296 is ever revisited.
sources: data/wins.json:066.detail_evidence (current): contains the trend-vs-static distinction but does not develop it as a separate failure layer.
Loeb et al. 2025 PNAS abstract (doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122): explicitly attributes the trend to time-varying drivers over 24 years.

stronger_arguments.description: Globe-fingerprint cross-leverage remains under-utilized (carry-forward from c5). The analyst's globe-fingerprint (reviewed 2026-04-09) documents the AE-projection NH:SH area ratio as 1:3 (NH inner-circle 3.15e8 km^2, SH annular-ring 9.45e8 km^2), distinct from the equal-area split our prose uses. The over-prediction critique survives under either mapping, but a defender attacking the "inner-half/outer-half is your construction" framing in detail_evidence has a small target. Adding a one-sentence note that the over-prediction holds across both equal-area and AE-equator mappings would close this. Optional; not currently a defender vector that has been exploited.
sources: monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-066.json (analyst, 2026-04-09): NH inner-circle 3.15e8 km^2, SH annular-ring 9.45e8 km^2, ratio 1:3.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-296 P1's three-counter defender-pivot structure (data/wins.json:066.detail_verdict_text) remains a strong piece of adversarial framing 21 days post-integration with no defender exploits surfacing. Counter (3)'s steel-trap — "own the inverse-cube integral and be wrong by 250-800×, or disown it and have nothing to cite" — continues to neutralize the most plausible defender retreat path. The Loeb 2025 verbatim quote in Counter (1) is the dome's own-citation-source attesting to non-dome mechanisms, which is a rhetorically clean move. The ISS-1906 / ISS-1908 / ISS-1917 closure cluster on 2026-05-09 produced TLDR text that leads with the over-prediction punchline (rather than the c3-era "can't produce hemisphere-dependent asymmetry" misframing). Per (data/wins.json:066.code_analysis), all four code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) remain accurate and stable.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Verdict remains Std Model Explains because (1) the dome contributes no new number per (data/wins.json:066.code_analysis): post_hoc=true and derives_from_dome=false; (2) the dome's geometry, taken seriously, predicts a wrong magnitude — per (data/wins.json:066.detail_evidence) the 90-270 W/m^2 / 0.34 W/m^2 ratio is ~250-800× too large (verified arithmetically: 90/0.34=264.7, 270/0.34=794.1); (3) the static dome geometry cannot produce a 24-year trend at all (Loeb 2025 mechanism quote in detail_evidence). The patch closes ISS-1917 as filed but the residual minor concerns this review flags (TLDR↔detail 200-vs-250 inconsistency, Counter 1 circularity, Counter 2 source verification, cross-WIN systemic) are quality-tightenings, not verdict threats.

deeper_analysis:
This c6 review was triggered by a fingerprint-drift signal that turned out to be a c5 fingerprint-update artifact, not real text change. The substantive content of WIN-066 (claim, finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) has been byte-stable since the workspace-sync at commit d22f6b13 on 2026-05-09T14:05Z. The c5 review at 22:46Z wrote tldr_*_length=371/193, but those values reflect the pre-d22f6b13 state, not the actual data at the time of c5. This is a c5 measurement bug, not a content drift. The audit's baseline is now accurate after this c6 update.

Substantively, the c5 review was thorough and most of its observations remain valid. The four minor c5 holes carry forward with minor refinements: (1) Counter (1) circularity risk persists unchanged; (2) Counter (2) source-verification need persists — raw-text V51 capture for WIN-066 still has not landed; (3) c5's ISS-1908 carry-over flag was a misread — the fix DID land at 13:20Z that day, the seasonal sun-orbit derivation (~7,400 to ~12,600 km) is present in current detail_evidence, and the issue has status=fixed in closed-issues.json; (4) cross-WIN sign-only systemic flag is queued via EXP-337-cross-win-sign-only-sweep.json (not yet integrated as of this review).

The new minor hole this cycle flags is the TLDR↔detail magnitude-ratio inconsistency: tldr_verdict says "200-800×" while detail_verdict_text says "~250-800×". The arithmetic favors the detail framing (90/0.34=264.7≈250, 270/0.34=794.1≈800). The TLDR's 200× understates the lower bound by 25%. This is a clean, mechanical fix — either align TLDR to "250-800×" or use a generic phrase. Both options are equally good rhetorically.

Defense_survives trajectory: c3 had defense_survives=3 (sign-non-discrimination + aether-damping + mapping). c4 had defense_survives=2. c5 had defense_survives=1 after EXP-296 P1 landed. c6 maintains defense_survives=1 — no new defender vectors have surfaced in 21 days of stable content, and the residual minor holes are quality-tightenings not defense escapes. The defender-pivot paragraph continues to function as a structural rebuttal.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-066 (Std Model Explains) and WIN-063 (also Std Model Explains, hemispheric magnetic dipole 3:1 ratio) are consistently framed — both rely on "dome has no derivation from canonical parameters" + "the underlying asymmetry has independent standard-physics explanation." WIN-066 vs WIN-067 (Self-Contradicted) verdict asymmetry remains defensible: WIN-067 has dome-stated H(r) coupling that self-contradicts on rim gravity; WIN-066 has no analogous dome-stated radiation formula to self-contradict against. WIN-058/-059/-060 sign-match cluster shares the recurring sign-non-discrimination pattern — EXP-337 will eventually sweep across all of them.

The recommended_action is "minor_edit" (not "no_change") because the TLDR↔detail 200-vs-250 inconsistency is a real, mechanical, fixable hole — small enough to bundle into the next decider self-apply cycle without an EXP.

citations_verified: Stephens et al. (2012, Nature Geoscience) doi:10.1038/ngeo1580 — verified in c2/c3/c5; unchanged.
Loeb et al. (2025, PNAS) doi:10.1073/pnas.2511595122 — verified in c3/c5; verbatim abstract quote in Counter (1) matches.

Quantitative ratio claims: 90/0.34=264.7×, 270/0.34=794.1×, confirming detail_verdict_text's "~250-800×" framing is arithmetically correct; tldr_verdict's "200-800×" lower bound (=68/0.34) is unsupported by the 90 W/m^2 floor stated in detail_evidence.
citations_unchecked: The dome page V51 WIN-066 verbatim text — raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt is still WIN-064-only as of 2026-05-08 capture; raw-text/02-wins.txt is V50.6 baseline 2026-03-12 (pre-WIN-066). Counter (2)'s assertion that the dome cites +0.34 explicitly is consistent with detail_claim and prior reviewer documentation but not directly verified against a verbatim V51 snapshot. Recommend poller capture WIN-066 on next scrape.

advocate_mode.best_defense: After 21 days of stable content with no defender exploits surfacing, the strongest remaining defense is a meta-rhetorical one against the TLDR↔detail inconsistency this review flagged: "Your own headline cites 200× as the minimum dome over-prediction; your derivation gives 250-265×. If you cannot keep your own numbers internally consistent across one WIN, why should anyone trust your 70-WIN review? This is the same hand-waving you accuse the dome of." This is purely cosmetic — it does not save the +0.34 vs 90-270 W/m^2 disparity, the post-hoc adoption, or the no-derivation dilemma. But it gives a sophisticated reader a free shot at our procedural rigor before they engage the substance. A defender quoting this on social media could embarrass the review without actually rebutting it. Severity rating: 2 (annoyance, not a verdict threat). The fix (tighten TLDR to 250-800×) costs zero rhetorical capital and removes the free shot.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Apply the minor_edit recommended above: tighten tldr_verdict's "200-800×" to "250-800×" (or use "hundreds of times the actual value" matching tldr_evidence). Cost: ~3 characters changed. Benefit: closes the meta-rhetorical procedural-rigor attack vector and aligns TLDR with detail.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-066 (Std Model Explains) vs WIN-067 (Self-Contradicted) verdict asymmetry on disc-geometry center-rim arguments remains defensible under our taxonomy as I analyzed in c4/c5: Self-Contradicted requires the dome's own equations to do the contradicting, and WIN-067 has H(r) coupling whereas WIN-066 lacks an analogous radiation-budget formula. WIN-066 and WIN-063 share "Std Model Explains + no dome derivation" framing — fully compatible. EXP-337-cross-win-sign-only-sweep is queued to address the WIN-058/-059/-060/-066 sign-match systemic pattern; no inconsistency introduced by waiting. No cross-WIN contradictions surfaced this cycle.

claims_checked: tldr_verdict ratio: "200-800×" — INCORRECT lower bound (200× corresponds to 68 W/m^2, but detail_evidence states the floor is 90 W/m^2; 90/0.34=264.7).
detail_verdict_text ratio: "~250-800×" — CORRECT (90/0.34=264.7 rounds to ~250; 270/0.34=794.1 rounds to ~800).

Seasonal sun-orbit radius range: ~7,400 km (summer NP) to ~12,600 km (winter solstice) — derivation cited in detail_evidence as the basis for the 90-270 W/m^2 range; consistent with c5's numerical integration.

Inverse-cube projected-flux falloff with h=5,733 km — geometrically correct; this is the dome's sun_altitude (canonical parameter), and standard inverse-square projection geometry with cosine projection gives r^(-3) effective fall-off.

NH:SH area ratios — equal-area split (used in prose, ~2.3× concentration ratio) vs AE-equator split (1:3 area ratio from globe-fingerprint) — both yield over-prediction by orders of magnitude as I noted in c5 stronger_arguments[0].
quantitative_verification.computations: Bash: python3 -c "print(f'{90/0.34:.1f}, {270/0.34:.1f}, {68/0.34:.1f}')" returned "264.7, 794.1, 200.0" — confirming that the "200×" lower bound in tldr_verdict corresponds to 68 W/m^2 not 90 W/m^2, while the "250-800×" lower bound in detail_verdict_text correctly anchors to 90 W/m^2. The tldr_verdict needs alignment.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: tldr_verdict's "200-800×" lower bound is inconsistent with both (a) the detail_verdict_text "250-800×" framing and (b) the detail_evidence-stated 90 W/m^2 floor. 200× understates the over-prediction by ~25%. This is the new minor hole flagged in holes_found[0].
